a year ago today, barcelona went dark. no electricity. no wifi. not even phone coverage.

and somehow… we felt more connected than ever.

people stopped working, scrolling, distracting themselves - and started talking. strangers shared music, food, conversations and candles. for a whole day, everyone was SO present.

it reminded me that when everything else disappears, human connection is what matters most, nothing can take that away from us.

let this be our reminder to appreciate and prioritize human connection a little more - text that friend, say yes to the plan, talk to the stranger, be present 🫶🏼

“Society doesn’t kill dreams overnight. It does it slowly.”
First they tell you to be realistic.
Then they laugh at your ideas.
Then they hand you a plan — college, career, mortgage — and call it the only way.

One day, you wake up and realize you traded your wild dreams for safety.
You traded passion for stability.
You stopped asking what if, and started asking what’s next.

But here’s the truth — dreamers aren’t broken, they’re just misunderstood.
They see color where others see grey.
They feel deeply, they crave freedom, they dare to believe life could be more.

So if you’ve ever been told your dreams are too big, too risky, too unrealistic —
remember, every great story started with someone refusing to fit in.

Keep creating. Keep wandering. Keep chasing that version of life that feels alive.
Because the world doesn’t need more people who follow the rules.
It needs more people brave enough to break them.
Nor(cole)man Rockwell

We spend our whole lives chasing “next.”
The next goal, the next job, the next trip, the next version of ourselves —
believing that’s where happiness finally lives.

But when we get there… it still doesn’t feel like enough.

You lose the weight.
You graduate.
You get the job.
You travel.
You find freedom —
and still, something inside whispers “what’s next?”

The truth is: no achievement, no amount of money, no level of freedom will ever fill the void if you lose touch with presence.

Because life isn’t waiting for you in the next milestone —
it’s happening right now.
In the quiet mornings, in the sunsets you barely notice,
in the version of life you once wished for.

Some spend a lifetime chasing what’s next.
I’m learning to slow down, breathe, and be here. 🫶
The city that time forgot.

I’m always asked this so here’s more info x

I budget everything & I really couldn’t care less about what I eat or where I sleep haha so I save a ton on food and accom (big one) and here’s some general tips;

- Accommodation is the easiest place to save money, if you’re ok staying in hostels (you spend 8 hours there so dw if it sucks haha)
- Most expensive part of traveling is getting to a new continent so I always buy my big flights in advance to save $$ and stick to one continent (SE Asia is soo cheap)
- The “experiences” I’m doing usually only cost $20-$40, It’s not like I’m bungie jumping or doing some retreat bs
- I was working the entire time, it looks like all I do is have fun but this is probably only 20% of my trip haha (in Malaysia I spent each day inside a coffee shop)

Here’s my average daily budget for ~2 months full of traveling (USD);
- Food: $12
- Accom: $17 (I paid $9 for a few hostels haha)
- Experiences: $12
- Flights: $950 flat (taking overnight buses saves hundreds)
- Bus, trains, etc: $4
- Visa: $55 (flat for 2)
- Esim: $2

Also… I hear the “you’re a creator you get free stuff” and that’s just false lmao. Over this 2 month period I got one thing for free and that was 3 nights in Singapore, outside of that I paid for everything myself. I didn’t even “stay at a buddy’s house for free” either haha

First time camping in the desert in Egypt 🐪

A bottle of water can cost almost nothing at home.
The same water can cost $5 at the airport.
$10 on a flight.
And somewhere in the middle of the desert, it can become priceless.

The water didn’t change.
The value changed because of the place.

And maybe that’s true for people too.

Maybe you’re not worthless.
Maybe you’ve just been trying to grow in places that were never meant for you.

Around people who don’t understand you.
In environments that drain you.
In routines that slowly make you forget who you are.

Sometimes changing your life doesn’t start with changing yourself completely.
Sometimes it starts with changing your surroundings.

New people.
New energy.
New nature.
New experiences.
A place where your mind can finally breathe again.

Because it’s hard to see your own value when you’re constantly surrounded by things that make you question it.

And I think traveling teaches you that.

You discover versions of yourself you never met before.
You become more confident.
More open.
More grateful.
You realize how small your fears were and how big the world actually is.

A different environment can change your mindset.
And a changed mindset can change your entire life.

So if you’ve been feeling lost lately, maybe the answer isn’t to hate yourself for it.
Maybe you just need a different place.
Different people.
Different conversations.
Different air.

Because flowers can’t grow in every environment.
And neither can people.

Sometimes growth begins the moment you leave what no longer feels like home.

Life is moving forward, just very slowly 🇺🇦

Life gets a lot easier when you stop making everything so complicated.

If you like someone, tell them.
If you miss someone, call them.
If you feel something deep inside your chest, do something about it instead of pretending it’s not there.

We spend so much time overthinking, waiting for the “perfect moment,” analyzing every little detail, and holding back words that could change everything. But life is actually so simple when you allow yourself to be honest.

Love people loudly.
Say what you mean.
Hug your friends longer.
Tell your parents you appreciate them.
Text the person you’ve been thinking about.
Take the trip.
Apologize when you’re wrong.
Forgive when you can.
Spread kindness even when the world feels cold.

Most of the things we regret are not the things we did.
They’re the things we felt but never acted on.

Life becomes lighter when you stop fighting your emotions and start listening to them instead.
Not every feeling needs to be hidden.
Not every connection needs to be played like a game.

Sometimes the best thing you can do is simply be genuine.

Because at the end of the day, people will remember how you made them feel.
The love you gave.
The energy you carried.
The positivity you spread.

So stop making life harder than it needs to be.
Feel deeply.
Love openly.
And if your heart tells you something matters…
have the courage to act on it.

Love doesn’t always have to be romantic.

Sometimes love is a friend noticing that you’ve gone quiet and asking if you’re really okay.

Sometimes it’s someone saving you a seat.
Someone walking on the outside of the sidewalk.
Someone sending you a message saying “text me when you get home.”

Sometimes love is a hand on your shoulder in a crowded room,
a long hug after a hard day,
or someone remembering the little things about you that you thought nobody noticed.

We’ve been taught to think love only matters when it’s flowers, dates, kisses, or relationships.

But some of the purest forms of love exist in the smallest moments.

A friend staying up late to listen to you talk.
Someone checking if you ate today.
A person believing in you when you can’t believe in yourself.

Love can sound like laughter in a kitchen.
Like “drive safe.”
Like “I’m proud of you.”
Like “you don’t have to go through this alone.”

And honestly,
some people have never been loved romantically,
but they have still been deeply loved.

By friends.
By family.
By strangers who showed kindness at the right moment.
By people who cared without expecting anything in return.

Not all love stories are romantic.
Some are simply about human connection.
About feeling seen.
About feeling safe around someone.

In a world where everyone is rushing,
being cared for softly is one of the rarest things.

So appreciate the people who check on you.
The people who sit with you in silence.
The people who make life feel a little lighter just by being there.

Because love is not only found in romance.

Sometimes,
love is simply someone making sure you’re okay.

Divide and conquer has always been the way those in power stay in power. Ordinary men and women have absolutely nothing to gain from being opponents.

Having grown up between cultures, religions, and identities, and having traveled to different countries and connected with communities all around the world, I can tell you one thing with certainty: all men want is to be loved. And all women want is to be loved.

Because love truly encapsulates everything. If you truly love someone, you’re honest with them. You treat them with respect, with kindness, with integrity. And I’m not talking about the love you have for a partner. I’m talking about the love and compassion you innately have for another human being.

We truly have nothing to gain from division.

Some argue that men benefit from division because we live in a patriarchal society. And while that is true I genuinely don’t think it serves men. 93% of people in prison are men. ￼60% of homeless people are men. ￼ Men are nearly twice as likely to have a substance abuse disorder. ￼ And 80% of suicides are men.

And the case for why division doesn’t serve women? I don’t think I even need to spell that one out. We see it every single day, in every single town, in every single country. 

At the end of the day, it’s up to each and every one of us to shift the narrative. And to love and care for one another. 

Never forget that we’re stronger together.

Sometimes the best thing you can do for yourself is to leave.
Leave the city that made you feel stuck.
Leave the routines that slowly drained your spark.
Leave the version of yourself that kept surviving instead of truly living.

Because the moment you step into the unknown, something changes inside you.

Travel doesn’t magically fix your life, but it changes the way you see it.
You start realizing how small your fears were compared to the world around you.
You meet people who live completely differently, yet somehow understand you better than the people you’ve known for years.
You learn that happiness isn’t always found in money, status, or doing what everyone expects from you.

Sometimes it’s found in a random sunset after a hard day.
In conversations with strangers that feel like home.
In laughing until your stomach hurts in places you can’t even pronounce.
In learning how to be alone without feeling lonely.

And somewhere along the journey, you start growing without even noticing it.

The things that used to break you don’t hit the same anymore.
You stop chasing validation.
You stop trying to fit into places you’ve outgrown.
You become calmer. Softer. Stronger. More yourself.

Travel teaches you that life is so much bigger than the small world you once lived in.
There’s more people to meet.
More dreams to chase.
More versions of yourself waiting to be discovered.

Sometimes leaving isn’t running away.
Sometimes leaving is finally choosing yourself.

And one day you’ll look back at the moment you were scared to take the leap…
and realize it was the moment your life actually started.

✨ interrupt complaining early
life’s not perfect, but complaining keeps you stuck there. Try to focus your energy on things you can actually change.

✨ take a step back before reacting
not every thought needs a reaction. Pause, breathe, notice your emotions and most of the time the negativity fades on its own.

✨ reframe the narrative
instead of assuming the worst, ask yourself what could actually go right. That shift alone changes everything!

✨ stop comparing
everyone’s dealing with things you don’t see. Comparison just creates pressure that isn’t real. 

negative thoughts aren’t the problem, feeding them is! 💛

One of the most valuable skills you can develop in life is the ability to reset fast.

Not because bad things won’t happen. They will.

People will disappoint you. Plans will fall apart. You’ll have bad days, make mistakes, face rejection, and experience moments that leave you questioning everything.

But the difference between people who keep moving forward and those who stay stuck isn’t what happens to them.

It’s how long they allow it to affect them.

You can’t control every situation. You can’t control what other people do. You can’t control every setback, loss, or challenge life throws your way.

But you can control your response.

You can choose whether a bad hour becomes a bad day. Whether a bad day becomes a bad week. Whether a setback becomes the story you tell yourself for the next year.

I’ve learned that resilience isn’t about never getting knocked down. It’s about getting back up quicker each time.

Feel the disappointment. Learn the lesson. Process the emotion.

Then reset.

Because life keeps moving, and the longer you stay attached to what went wrong, the more you miss what’s still waiting for you ahead.

The people who thrive aren’t the ones with perfect lives.

They’re the ones who refuse to let temporary problems become permanent obstacles.

Reset. Refocus. Keep going.

Your future doesn’t care about what happened yesterday. It cares about what you do next.

Society will try to hand you a script before you even discover who you are.

Go to school.
Get a job.
Work nine to five.
Follow the timeline.
Buy the house.
Stay realistic.
Don’t take risks.
Don’t be too different.
Don’t dream too big.

And somewhere along the way, so many people stop listening to themselves because they become too busy listening to what the world expects from them.

But your soul was not created just to survive every week waiting for two days of freedom.

Your soul wants to feel alive.

Maybe that means traveling the world with a backpack and no perfect plan.
Maybe it means waking up next to the ocean instead of in traffic.
Maybe it means creating art, helping people, starting over, chasing freedom, laughing more, loving deeper, or simply living slower.

Not everyone will understand your path.
Some people are so trapped in society’s expectations that they think happiness only exists inside approval, money, status, and routines.

But there are people with expensive watches who feel empty inside.
And there are people sitting on a beach with almost nothing who feel rich in ways money can never buy.

Because peace is priceless.
Freedom is priceless.
Being able to wake up excited for your life is priceless.

At the end of your life, nobody else will feel your regrets for you.
Nobody else will carry the weight of the dreams you ignored just to fit into someone else’s version of success.

So satisfy your soul, not society.

Travel if your heart wants to explore.
Leave places that drain you.
Be kind to strangers.
Spend time with people who make you feel human again.
Take care of your body.
Watch sunsets.
Create memories.
Laugh loudly.
Love deeply.
Live fully.

You were not born just to pay bills and die with a schedule in your hand.

You were born to experience life.

You are free to choose your own reality.
You are free to decide who you become.

The person who wakes up at 5am every day was once lazy.
The confident person was once insecure.
The disciplined athlete once had no motivation.
The calm person once had chaos in their mind.

People act like personality is permanent, like who you are today is who you’ll always be. But that’s not true. Human beings are built to change, grow, adapt, heal, and evolve.

You can become someone completely different in a year.
Not by pretending.
Not overnight.
But through small decisions repeated every single day.

The reality you live in is often created by your habits, your mindset, the people around you, and the story you keep telling yourself. If you keep telling yourself you’re weak, lost, lazy, awkward, broken, or incapable… eventually you’ll believe it. But if you start choosing different actions, different environments, and different thoughts, your entire life can slowly change.

You don’t have to stay the version of yourself that survived hard times.
You’re allowed to become the version that thrives.

You can choose health.
You can choose peace.
You can choose discipline.
You can choose adventure.
You can choose to leave old habits behind.
You can choose to rebuild yourself from nothing if you have to.

And the beautiful thing is — nobody can stop you from becoming who you truly want to be except yourself.

Your past explains you.
It does not define you.

So if you feel stuck right now, remember this:
the person you dream about becoming already exists inside you.
You just have to make choices that align with that version of yourself, over and over again, until one day you wake up and realize… you became them.

Here are the 7️⃣ things I wish someone told me before I started making content:

1️⃣ You don’t need a mic, a team, a drone, or even a tripod. I bought my first tripod yesterday after 3 years of making content.

2️⃣ Most people feel misunderstood. Make them feel seen, they’ll follow you.

3️⃣ Don’t gatekeep. Someone’s already given your “secret strategy” away for free. So be honest with your audience and give them real value.

4️⃣ Ditch “follow for more tips.” Instead, use a CTA that relates to your video, gives people a reason to follow and makes them feel like it was their decision to follow you.

5️⃣ Make people feel something. Angry, nostalgic, confused, inspired, etc. When people feel they follow

6️⃣ Say the stuff no one else is brave enough to. Be controversial and stand up for what you believe in. 

7️⃣ S-I-R. Statement. Information. Resolution. Structure every video around this and watch your retention climb.

The first to apologize is often the bravest.

Not because they’re wrong.
Not because they have all the answers.
But because they’re willing to put their ego aside for something greater than being right.

The first to forgive is the strongest.

Because forgiveness isn’t weakness. It’s choosing not to carry the weight of anger, resentment, and pain any longer. It’s deciding that what happened to you will not control your future.

And the first to forget is often the happiest.

Not because they don’t remember what happened, but because they refuse to relive it every day. They understand that life is too short to keep reopening old wounds.

One of the most powerful things you can learn in life is that holding onto hurt doesn’t punish the other person—it punishes you. The anger, the bitterness, the endless replaying of the situation in your mind only steals your peace, your energy, and your happiness.

Forgive them.

Not because they deserve another chance.
Not because what they did was okay.
Not because you have to let them back into your life.

Forgive them because you deserve peace.

You deserve to wake up without carrying yesterday’s pain.
You deserve to move forward without dragging old baggage behind you.
You deserve a heart that feels light instead of heavy.

Some people will never apologize.
Some people will never understand how much they hurt you.
Some people will never become the person you hoped they would be.

But your healing doesn’t need their permission.

Choose peace over pride.
Choose growth over resentment.
Choose freedom over bitterness.

Because at the end of the day, the person who benefits most from forgiveness is not them.

It’s you. ❤️

One day, you’ll be old.

Your hair might be grey, your body a little slower, and your passport tucked away in a drawer collecting dust. And when you look back on your life, I promise you won’t wish you had spent more time scrolling, worrying, or waiting for the “perfect” moment.

You’ll wish you had gone.

You’ll wish you had booked that flight.

You’ll wish you had watched more sunsets in places you couldn’t pronounce, gotten lost in unfamiliar streets, met strangers who became unforgettable memories, and experienced just how big and beautiful this world really is.

Because the truth is, the perfect time never comes.

There will always be another bill to pay, another reason to wait, another excuse to stay comfortable. But life has a way of passing by faster than we think.

Travel isn’t just about seeing new places. It’s about discovering new versions of yourself. It’s about realizing how small your problems can feel when you’re standing on top of a mountain, watching the ocean stretch endlessly before you, or sharing a meal with people who don’t even speak your language.

The world has so much to teach you if you’re willing to step outside your comfort zone.

Years from now, you won’t regret the trips you took. You’ll remember the adventures, the laughter, the mistakes, the people, the stories, and the moments that made you feel truly alive.

But you might regret the journeys you never started.

So go.

Take the trip.

See the world.

Because one day, when you’re looking back on your life, you’ll be grateful for every stamp in your passport, every risk you took, and every moment you chose experience over fear.

The world is waiting for you. Don’t wait forever to meet it. 🌍✈️

the ocean is the ultimate life support system for our planet. 

50% of the oxygen we breathe is generated by the ocean, and it absorbs much of the carbon dioxide and excess heat generated by human activity. it’s also home to around 80% of Earth's biodiversity🐳

🐬 support ethical animal encounters- don’t chase them down and avoid captive marine exhibits

We live in a world that constantly makes us feel late.

Late to become successful.
Late to find love.
Late to build a business.
Late to buy a house.
Late to figure out who we are.

Every day, social media shows us people getting rich at 22, getting married at 25, traveling the world, buying dream cars, and living what looks like a perfect life.

And without even realizing it, we start comparing our behind-the-scenes to someone else’s highlight reel.

We begin to feel like we’re falling behind.

But what if we’re not?

What if the grass isn’t always greener on the other side?

What if the people you’re comparing yourself to are struggling too, just in ways you can’t see through a screen?

What if real life is actually better than the dream social media sold us?

A slow morning with a coffee.
A walk with a friend.
A sunset you didn’t post.
A laugh that wasn’t recorded.
A simple life filled with peace, health, and people you love.

Maybe that’s the stuff that actually matters.

Maybe life was never meant to be a race.

Maybe you don’t need to have everything figured out right now.

You don’t need to be where someone else is.
You don’t need to follow someone else’s timeline.
You don’t need to rush your life because the internet told you to.

Your journey is yours.

So take a breath, stop comparing, and look around.

You might realize that the life you’re chasing online isn’t nearly as beautiful as the life you’re already living.
Not many people make it this far

YOU ATTRACT WHAT YOU THINK.

Not because life magically gives you everything you imagine, but because your thoughts shape the way you see the world.

The same sunrise can feel like a burden to one person and a blessing to another.

One person wakes up and thinks,
“I have to wake up this early.”

Another wakes up and thinks,
“I get to wake up today.”

Same morning.
Same alarm.
Completely different mindset.

The thoughts you feed every day become the lens through which you experience your life.

If you constantly focus on what is missing, you’ll find more reasons to feel empty.
If you constantly focus on what is going wrong, you’ll notice more problems than possibilities.

But when you choose gratitude, even for the small things, something changes.

You stop saying,
“I have to go to work,”
and start saying,
“I get to earn a living.”

You stop saying,
“I have to exercise,”
and start saying,
“I get to move a body that works.”

You stop saying,
“I have to face another day,”
and start saying,
“I get another day.”

Your life may not change overnight.

But your experience of life will.

The truth is that most of us spend so much time waiting for our circumstances to improve before allowing ourselves to feel happy.

But happiness often starts long before the circumstances change.

It starts with the thoughts you choose to believe.

Your mind is a garden.

Whatever you water will grow.

Water fear, doubt, and negativity, and they’ll take over.

Water gratitude, hope, and possibility, and you’ll begin to see opportunities where you once saw obstacles.

The world is not always positive.
Life is not always easy.

But there is power in choosing where you place your focus.

Because what you think about every day eventually becomes the reality you live in.

So tomorrow morning, when your alarm goes off, remember:

You don’t have to wake up.

You get to wake up.

And that small shift might change everything.

Life becomes so much lighter when you stop trying to be perfect and start trying to learn.

Learn from your mistakes instead of letting them define you.
Learn from your failures instead of carrying them as regrets.
Learn from your pain instead of turning it into anger.

Not every situation needs a fight.
Not every disagreement needs a raised voice.
Sometimes the strongest thing you can do is stay calm when chaos expects a reaction.

Relax instead of yelling.
Listen instead of assuming.
Forgive instead of hating.

Because hate only keeps you connected to the thing that hurt you.
Forgiveness doesn’t mean what happened was okay. It means you’re choosing peace over carrying the weight forever.

The truth is that we are all learning as we go.
We all make mistakes.
We all say things we wish we could take back.
We all have moments we’re not proud of.

But growth begins the moment you stop asking, “Why did this happen to me?” and start asking, “What can this teach me?”

A better life isn’t built by never making mistakes.
A better life is built by learning from them.

Be patient with yourself.
Be kind to others.
Choose understanding over judgment.
Choose growth over ego.
Choose peace over anger.

At the end of the day, the people who become the strongest aren’t the ones who never fall down.

They’re the ones who keep getting back up with a little more wisdom, a little more compassion, and a little more love in their hearts every time.

You become what you practice every single day.

Not what you talk about.
Not what you dream about at 2am.
Not what you say you’ll “start next Monday.”

You become what you repeatedly do.

If you wake up every day and train, your body will adapt.
If you create every day, your mind will adapt.
If you show up consistently, even when nobody is watching, eventually your life adapts too.

People spend so much time waiting to “feel ready” before becoming the person they want to be.
But confidence is built after the action, not before it.

The runner became a runner by running.
The athlete became an athlete by training.
The creator became a creator by posting when nobody cared yet.

Nobody starts as the finished version of themselves.

At first it feels awkward.
You feel behind.
You compare yourself to people who have done it for years.
You feel like an imposter pretending to be something you’re not.

But every expert was once the beginner nobody believed in.

And the truth is, reality listens to repetition.
Your habits slowly shape your identity.
Every workout.
Every early morning.
Every uncomfortable step.
Every time you choose discipline over excuses.

That is what changes your life.

Because action is stronger than self-doubt.
Action is stronger than fear.
Action is stronger than the voice in your head telling you that you can’t.

You don’t need permission to become someone new.
You don’t need to wait for the “perfect moment.”
You don’t need everyone else to believe in you first.

You just need to start.

Again.
And again.
And again.

One day you’ll look back and realize the person you wanted to become was built in the small decisions nobody else noticed.

So if there’s something you dream about becoming…
start acting like that person today.

Because eventually, reality catches up to consistency.

I think we overestimate the risk and underestimate how much staying the same costs 💌

I went from 2,646 to 1.2 Million followers by doing one thing: Showing up every day.

💬 Comment “Build” and I’ll send you my free social media guidebook 

Here’s the strategy breakdown:

1️⃣ Study what works
- Follow 10-20 content creators in your niche with 10-200K followers.
- Analyse their top 20 reels + watch their recent content. See what works and what doesn’t
- Use these viral formats as inspiration and input your own personality / story

2️⃣ Use a CTA (Call to Action)
- If your video is 10-30s long CTA at the end
- If your video is 30s+ CTA in the middle
- Be specific and creative. DO NOT use generic CTAs like, “follow for more travel tips”

3️⃣ Writing Captions
- Insert information/ personal story/ value in the caption to increase video watch time.
- Anything you wanted to say in the video but didn’t have time… put it in the caption
- Make them easy to read. Base level English.

4️⃣ Learn + practise storytelling
- Use the S-I-R Method. Statement - information - Resolution. In that order.
- Use your analytics to see where people are dropping off.
- Practise implementing a second hook.

5️⃣ Post Consistently
- Aim for 5-7 reels a week minimum.
- Post every day around the same time. Creates trust + anticipation.
- The algorithm rewards consistency. 

Most people quit around the 3 month mark. I nearly did. 3 months of posting 7 times a week and only 150 new followers. But I kept going, and two weeks later one video got me 30K followers.

Three years ago I left Ireland with €6,000 and a one way ticket. No backup plan, no rich parents to bail me out and no experience in any of this. 

But I wanted ‘out’ of my life at the time. 

I’d never made a reel until 2 days before I left for this trip, it was a bit crazy now that I think about it. But I figured I could make €6,000 last for about 6 months if I went to Southeast Asia, so that’s what I did.

I was fully aware I could crash and burn and end up back home at 24 with nothing to show for it. And honestly? That almost happened.

Three months in I was sitting in a hospital bed in Malaysia with €50 to my name wondering what on earth I was doing with my life (I was owed €3,500 from my insurance company, so I knew I technically had money, but I was still alone in Malaysia with only €50 😅).

But I kept going, landed my first brand deal (€60 to be a model for a soap company), then another (€300 to promote a travel pillow company), then another.

And before I knew it, in 2025 I got paid €15,000 for ONE video (can’t name the brand but it’s a big one!)

So three years on from taking the biggest risk of my life, I’m 27, I have 1.2 million followers on Instagram, 400K on Facebook and 185K on TikTok.

For the last six months I’ve been quietly putting together everything I’ve actually figured out about how this whole thing works. Tested it with a small group first to make sure it wasn’t just luck for me.

Some of the things keeping you awake at night will never happen.

Some of the conversations you’re replaying in your head weren’t as important as you think.
Some of the fears you’re carrying belong to a future that doesn’t even exist yet.

We spend so much time trying to control every outcome, predict every problem, and prepare for every possible disappointment that we forget to actually live.

Peace isn’t found when life becomes perfect.
Peace is found when you stop demanding certainty from an uncertain world.

Not every question needs an answer today.
Not every decision needs to be rushed.
Not every setback means you’re on the wrong path.

Sometimes the most powerful thing you can do is trust that you’ll handle whatever comes when it arrives.

Go for the walk.
Watch the sunset.
Call the friend.
Take the trip.
Laugh a little louder.
Be present for the life that’s happening right now.

Because while you’re busy worrying about tomorrow, today is quietly becoming a memory.

Protect your peace.
Not everything deserves your attention.
Not everything deserves your energy.
And not everything deserves a place in your mind.

Life feels a lot lighter when you stop carrying problems that don’t exist yet.

Most people are mentally living everywhere except the present moment.

One second they’re stressed about the future.
The next they’re angry about the past.
Then they open social media and absorb thousands of opinions, fears, rumors, fake lifestyles, bad news, drama, and negativity from people they’ve never even met.

And slowly…
they stop living their own life.

The world will always be loud.
The news will always find something to make you afraid.
Social media will always try to convince you that you’re behind, missing out, not good enough, not rich enough, not successful enough.

But real life is happening outside the screen.

It’s in the late night talks with people you love.
The sunsets you almost missed because you were scrolling.
The workouts that make you feel alive.
The random laughs.
The road trips.
The music in your headphones while walking alone.
The peace of waking up and realizing you don’t need to prove yourself to strangers online.

You were not born to spend your entire life reacting to everybody else’s opinions.

Go outside.
Touch reality again.
Breathe fresh air.
Create memories instead of just consuming content.
Stop carrying the weight of every rumor, every headline, every comment section.

Most of it won’t matter in a few years.
Some of it won’t even matter tomorrow.

Protect your peace.
Protect your mind.
Live in the moment.
Because one day you’ll realize life was never waiting for you on your phone screen…

it was happening right in front of you the whole time.

what if?

you have the ability to change your life…if you choose / want too. you don’t have to stay static. 

im not saying you need to travel the world full time to do it either- BUT since i’ve changed my environments I’ve been able to discover more about myself and what I want out of life. Places that make me feel more in alignment with who i am and who I want to become. the values and people i want to surround myself with.

i changed my habits. i saved up money, moved out and starting traveling on my own. i’m constantly learning and working towards the life i want to live and doing so unapologetically, cause life’s too short not to at least try❤️‍🔥

you can always go back to what you know, but you will never find out who you could be unless you start switching it up 🦋

Sometimes it takes being at rock bottom to find the motivation to climb to the top. Being in the middle is safe, it’s not exactly comfortable but it’s not uncomfortable enough for you to do anything about it. 

Mentally I was pretty close to rock bottom when I quit my job and physically I was rock bottom when I was in hospital in Malaysia. And some part of me just said, “ENOUGH”. 

I pulled myself together and worked really really hard to achieve my goal. And if I can do it so can you 🫶🏼
